commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is indicated; this maintainer draft needs maintainer acceptance of the experimental lean-mode compatibility change and a successful broad changed gate before landing.; Cleared: The diff only changes lean-mode TypeScript filtering, focused tests, and docs; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, publishing, or code-download surfaces. (sha=3c228eedeafc2e12abe0e22402927e5eb74a5b1a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch expands `localModelLean` filtering to deny media-generation, TTS, and PDF tools, adds focused lean-mode tests, and updates local-model/Ollama docs.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +52, Docs 0. Total +61 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a behavior-tuning PR rather than a reproduced bug report. Source inspection does show current main trims only `browser`, `cron`, and `message`, while the branch adds the broader trim.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is indicated; this maintainer draft needs maintainer acceptance of the experimental lean-mode compatibility change and a successful broad changed gate before landing.

target.title: fix(agents): trim media tools in lean mode
target.body:
## Summary
- trim additional heavyweight optional tools from `localModelLean`: media generation, voice, and PDF alongside browser, cron, and message
- keep image-understanding available so lean local agents can still inspect inbound images when configured
- document the broader lean-mode surface and the explicit `tools.allow` preservation path

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts --reporter=dot`: passed, 1 shard / 10 tests
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/concepts/experimental-features.md docs/gateway/local-models.md docs/providers/ollama.md`: passed
- `node scripts/docs-list.js`: passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/local-model-lean.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts docs/concepts/experimental-features.md docs/gateway/local-models.md docs/providers/ollama.md`: passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts`: passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`: passed
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: attempted with `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --label local-model-lean-media-trim-check-changed --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`, but coordinator returned `active lease limit exceeded: 9/8` before allocation; no lease was created.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: lean local-model agents should expose a smaller direct tool surface now that OpenClaw includes broader optional media, voice, and PDF tools.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw gwt worktree rebased onto current `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: targeted local-model lean Vitest coverage, docs MDX check, docs list script, oxfmt, oxlint, diff-check, and attempted AWS Crabbox changed gate.

Evidence after fix: `filterLocalModelLeanTools` removes `image_generate`, `music_generate`, `video_generate`, `tts`, and `pdf` in lean mode while preserving `image` understanding and honoring explicit preserved tool names/groups.

Observed result after fix: lean mode keeps the normal coding/conversation path available with fewer heavyweight optional schemas in the prompt; explicit allowlists can still opt an agent back into removed tools.

What was not tested: remote `pnpm check:changed`, blocked by AWS Crabbox lease capacity.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: fix(cli): show Gemini CLI runtime auth status
target.body:
## Summary
- include CLI runtime auth providers in `models status` runtime-auth accounting
- report canonical `google/*` text/default routes using `google-gemini-cli` as runtime auth routes while keeping image routes on Google API-key auth
- update Gemini CLI OAuth setup to write the existing provider/model-scoped `agentRuntime` policy on the default Google model instead of the ignored whole-agent runtime key
- keep CLI runtime auth checks provider-specific so `claude-cli` is not considered usable from canonical Anthropic auth/env that the CLI runtime clears
- keep CLI runtime health scoped to runtime auth so stale canonical provider OAuth cannot fail a usable CLI-runtime route
- add regression coverage for `GEMINI_API_KEY` plus Gemini CLI OAuth profile, login-generated config patching, plugin-owned CLI runtime synthetic auth, missing native CLI runtime auth, and stale canonical-provider health beside usable CLI runtime auth

Fixes #79585.

## Surface area
- No public config schema changes.
- No plugin API or plugin contract changes.
- No new CLI flags, env vars, migrations, or setup surfaces.
- This only changes `models status` auth reporting/checking and the existing Gemini CLI OAuth setup patch target.

## Refresh note
Rebased onto `main` `53a7545ae3ef23d90926ae4f1585df5cbcc0f602`.

Signed head: `a4926c598c496c4621d02bf275978e1fcbed407e`.

## Follow-up fixes after review
- Fixed `no-shadow` by renaming the local runtime-resolution variable.
- Added CLI runtime providers to synthetic-auth probing, so plugin-owned runtime auth such as `claude-cli` is detected for runtime routes.
- Kept runtime-route checks direct/provider-specific, so canonical Anthropic auth or env does not make a `claude-cli` route pass when native Claude CLI auth is missing.
- Addressed Codex review by not expanding CLI runtime health through deprecated canonical auth aliases.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 2 files, 39 tests passed
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts extensions/google/gemini-cli-provider.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts` -> passed
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts extensions/google/gemini-cli-provider.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed
- `/home/giodl/.local/toolchains/node-v22.22.3-linux-x64/bin/codex review --base origin/main` -> no actionable correctness issues found
- `git log --show-signature --format="%H %G? %s" origin/main..HEAD` -> all 5 commits have good signatures

## Known external gate state
After rebasing, the broader `pnpm lint`, `pnpm tsgo:prod`, and `pnpm check:test-types` failures reproduce in `extensions/phone-control/*`, which this PR does not modify. The PR diff remains scoped to:
- `src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts`
- `src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts`
- `extensions/google/index.test.ts`

## Remaining proof gap
I still have not run live Gemini CLI OAuth login plus `models status --check` on this machine. The branch now has the static/focused coverage and Codex review clean, but the ClawSweeper live-proof request remains unless a maintainer accepts the remaining risk or live Gemini CLI OAuth proof is produced.

No merge performed.
